prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The 65th Annual Grammy Awards ceremony, held at the Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles on February 5, 2023, was a music awards ceremony that honored the best recordings, compositions, and artists of each year from October 1, 2021 to September 30, 2022 as determined by members of the National Academy of Recording Arts and Sciences. The announcement was made on November 15, 2022. South African comedian Trevor Noah, who hosted the 63rd and 64th ceremonies, returned. Beyoncé was the most nominated artist (four), followed followed by Kendrick
B. The 65th yearly Grammy Awards gala, an event dedicated to recognizing musical achievements, took place on February 5, 2023, at the Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles. This ceremony honored the top recordings, musical arrangements, and performers from the qualifying period spanning October 1, 2021, through September 30, 2022. The selection process was carried out by members of the National Academy of Recording Arts and Sciences. The list of nominees was publicly revealed on November 15, 2022. Trevor Noah, a comedian from South Africa who previously presided over both the 63rd and 64th ceremonies, once again assumed the role of host. Beyoncé garnered the highest number of nominations, receiving nine, and achieved a tie with Maverick City Music for the most wins, each securing four. Kendrick Lamar followed with eight nominations and three victories, while Adele and Brandi Carlile each accumulated seven nominations. Beyoncé, with a cumulative total of 88 nominations throughout her career, reached an equal standing with her husband, Jay-Z, as the individual with the most nominations.
C. The 65th Annual Grammy Awards ceremony was a music awards ceremony held at the Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles on February 5, 2023. It recognized the best recordings, compositions, and artists of the eligibility year – October 1, 2021 to September 30, 2022 – as determined by the members of National Academy of Recording Arts and Sciences. The nominations were announced on November 15, 2022. South African comedian Trevor Noah, who hosted the 63rd and 64th ceremonies, returned again.Beyoncé received the most nominations (nine) and tied Maverick City Music for wins (four), followed by Kendrick Lamar with eight nominations and three wins, and Adele and Brandi Carlile with seven nominations each. With a career total of 88 nominations, Beyoncé tied with her husband Jay-Z as the most nominated

Answer:
